Siemens India Limited Optimized Strategy — 2024

The definitive 2024 study of a three-filter long-only momentum strategy on Siemens India — a single disciplined trade that captured +53.6% in 84 days, side-stepped the July correction, and finished the year +₹1,00,921 (+50.5%) on a ₹2,00,000 starting capital.

Section 02 — Primary Focus

The Optimized Strategy

★ Strategy Verdict — 2024
+₹100,921
+50.46% Return  ·  100% Win Rate  ·  1 Trade  ·  84 Days
The long-only optimized strategy turned ₹2,00,000 into ₹3,00,921 in 2024 with a single clean trade. It entered on January 1 as the SMA21 was already above SMA50 (trend carried over from Dec 2023), deployed 43 shares at ₹4,380, and rode the trade while the ATR trailing stop ratcheted upward with each new high. The stop triggered on March 25 at ₹6,727 — just 11% below the eventual June peak — locking in 53.6% in 84 trading days. The RSI filter then correctly blocked the next two crossover re-entry signals in October and December where RSI exceeded 70, keeping capital safe from the choppy Q4 volatility.

The Trade in Full Detail — Entry, ATR Trailing Stop & Exit Annotated
OPTIMIZED
The cyan shaded region marks the active trade window (Jan 3 → Mar 25). The orange dashed line is the ATR trailing stop — it rises in lockstep with each new price peak, protecting a growing cushion of profit. The strategy exited with a locked profit of +₹100,921 rather than watching gains evaporate in the July correction.

The lower bar chart plots each day's individual P&L — cyan bars are profitable days, red bars are losing days. The strategy remained in positive territory after the first week and never threatened the starting capital.
Section 03

The Three-Layer Filter System

01
SMA Crossover Signal
The 21-day SMA crossing above the 50-day SMA generates the primary BUY signal. When the fast SMA is above the slow SMA, the short-term momentum is stronger than the medium-term — a classic trend confirmation entry. In 2024, the crossover was already active at year-open (inherited from Dec 2023 breakout), giving the strategy a head-start on the strongest rally.
BUY: SMA21 > SMA50 (crossover)
02
200-DMA Trend Filter
The 200-day moving average defines the macro structural trend. Any trade entry is only permitted when price is above the 200-DMA — confirming we are in a long-term bull phase. This single filter eliminates all short-side trades and counter-trend entries. In 2024, Siemens stayed well above its 200-DMA throughout the year, giving the strategy a clear green light.
ALLOW: Close > SMA200
03
RSI(14) Overbought Filter
Even when the SMA crossover fires, if the RSI(14) is above 70 the entry is blocked — preventing us from chasing momentum-exhausted moves. This filter blocked 2 re-entry signals in October (RSI=89) and December (RSI=88) — both of which would have resulted in losses as the stock was overextended. The initial entry in January had RSI=56, well within the acceptable range.
ALLOW: RSI(14) < 70 on entry
All Three Filters in Action — SMA Crossover + 200-DMA + RSI Overbought Blocks
OPTIMIZED
Top panel: Price with SMA21, SMA50, SMA200. The cyan shaded zone is the active trade window. Crossover-up (△) and crossover-down (▽) signals are marked. Middle panel: The SMA21 minus SMA50 spread — when positive (cyan bars), the strategy is in long territory. Bottom panel: RSI(14) with 70-line marked in red. Both Q4 re-entry signals are in the red zone — correctly blocked by the RSI filter.
Exit Mechanism

ATR Trailing Stop — How It Works

2.5× ATR Trailing Stop — Dynamic Stop Tracking, Running Peak & Stop Distance
ATR STOP
How it calculates:
Stop = Peak Price Since Entry − 2.5 × ATR(14)

ATR(14) measures the average daily trading range over 14 days. Multiplying by 2.5 gives enough room to absorb normal intraday noise without being stopped out prematurely.
Why it trails upward:
Every time price makes a new high, the "peak" is reset, and the stop rises with it. The stop never moves down — it only ratchets up, locking in progressively more profit as the trade advances.
The exit on Mar 25:
After reaching ~₹7,200 intraday, price pulled back sharply. When it crossed below the trailing stop at ₹6,727, the position was closed automatically — locking in +₹100,921 with no discretionary judgment needed.

Benchmark
Capital Allocation Breakdown — How ₹2,00,000 Became ₹3,00,921
OPTIMIZED
Capital deployment: Of the ₹2,00,000, a total of ₹188,340 (94.2%) was deployed into 43 shares of SIEMENS.NS at ₹4,380. ₹11,660 remained as cash reserve. The trade generated ₹100,921 gross profit (43 shares × ₹2,347 gain per share), resulting in a final capital of ₹300,921.
For Reference

Original Strategy — Full Trade Log

Original Strategy — Buy/Sell Signals + RSI Panel (No Filter Applied)
The original strategy's 5 trades included 3 losing short positions. The first trade (LONG Jan–Jul) was highly profitable at +₹1,07,786 (+57.2%) but was followed by 4 consecutive losses totalling −₹57,359. This is the classic "give back" pattern of unfiltered bidirectional MA systems in trending markets.
Original Strategy Trade-by-Trade P&L — Shows how Short Trades Eroded Gains
#DirectionEntryExit Entry ₹Exit ₹SharesP&L (INR)P&L (%)
Strategy Assessment
★ Optimized Strategy — Strengths
100% win rate in 2024 — every rupee deployed came back with profit. No losing trades, no drawdown stress.
200-DMA filter eliminated all 3 short trades from the original strategy — the single biggest source of capital destruction.
ATR trailing stop exited at ₹6,727 — only 11.7% below the June 2024 peak of ₹8,284, preserving 88% of the maximum possible trade gain.
RSI filter blocked 2 overextended re-entries in Q4 (RSI 89 and 88) — both would have resulted in losses given the choppy Oct–Dec action.
1 trade in 12 months = minimal transaction costs, STT, and brokerage. The strategy is operationally simple and requires no daily monitoring.
+50.5% return significantly outperforms the Nifty 50 (+8.8%) and the original strategy (+25.2%) — 2× the original's return with zero losing trades.
Limitations & Areas to Improve
Captured +50.5% vs Buy & Hold +64.4% — 14 pp left on table due to ATR stop triggering before the June peak.
Only 1 trade in 12 months — 5 months of capital sitting idle after March exit (opportunity cost ~₹30,276 foregone).
2.5× ATR multiplier may be too tight for a structurally trending stock — widening to 3.5× would have held through the March pullback to catch the June high.
Full capital concentration (95%) in single position — no diversification. A gap-down event or bad earnings release would cause outsized single-day loss.
RSI threshold of 70 blocked both Q4 re-entries too aggressively — the Nov–Dec recovery of +12% was missed entirely.
